title: SUPPORTIVE SERVICES FOR VETERAN FAMILIES - 211info
text: COMMUNITY ACTION TEAM SUPPORTIVE SERVICES FOR VETERAN FAMILIES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers short-term housing assistance. Support may include help with finding housing, move-in or rental assistance, eviction prevention, and access to benefits, employment, education, legal services related to housing barriers, and other expenses that support stable housing. Case management is available, pending eligibility. Participation requires working with a caseworker, and some programs may require individuals to be drug- and alcohol-free. All assistance is based on eligibility and the availability of funding. 125 N 17th Street Saint Helens, OR 97051 Eligibility: Veterans and their families who are homeless or at risk of becoming homeless and have an income at or below 80% of Area Median Income with any discharge other than Dishonorable or General Court Martial. Veteran must be present in the household. Hours: Monday-Friday 8:30am-12:30pm and 1:30pm-5pm Email (503) 397-3511 Get Directions Visit Website Phone/FAX Numbers Intake (503) 397-3511 Email Address: [email protected] INTAKE PROCEDURE: Visit website or call for information FEES: None DOCUMENTS: DD214, photo ID, Social Security card, proof of income AREA SERVED: Clatsop, Columbia, and Tillamook Counties SITE HOURS: Monday-Friday 8:30am-12:30pm and 1:30pm-5pm Services: Case/Care Management * Homeless Veterans Case/Care Management * Veterans Homeless Permanent Supportive Housing * Veterans Housing Related Coordinated Entry * Veterans Rent Payment Assistance * Homeless Veterans Rent Payment Assistance * Veterans Rental Deposit Assistance * Homeless Veterans Rental Deposit Assistance * Veterans Other Services Offered At This Location: COMMUNITY GROUPS AND GOVERNMENT/ADMINISTRATIVE OFFICES - AREA AGENCY ON AGING COMMUNITY GROUPS AND GOVERNMENT/ADMINISTRATIVE OFFICES - COMMUNITY ACTION AGENCY FOOD - HOME DELIVERED MEALS HEALTH SUPPORTIVE SERVICES - AGING AND DISABILITY RESOURCE CONNECTION HOUSING/SHELTER - HOUSING PROGRAMS HOUSING/SHELTER - HOUSING REHABILITATION PROGRAM INDIVIDUAL AND FAMILY SUPPORT SERVICES - OPTIONS COUNSELING INDIVIDUAL AND FAMILY SUPPORT SERVICES - OREGON PROJECT INDEPENDENCE INDIVIDUAL AND FAMILY SUPPORT SERVICES - OREGON PROJECT INDEPENDENCE – MEDICAID INFORMATION SERVICES - FAMILY CAREGIVER SUPPORT PROGRAM LEGAL SERVICES - VETERANS SERVICES OFFICE MENTAL HEALTH ASSESSMENT AND TREATMENT - RCI REACH MONEY MANAGEMENT - MONEY MANAGEMENT PROGRAM UTILITIES - UTILITY ASSISTANCE PROGRAM If you represent this agency, update your listing here . * The community resource directory information is up to date to the best of our knowledge. However, you should always call the provider to confirm this information and make an appointment. Be sure to confirm payment information with the provider, if payment is required. 211Info does not rate, recommend or endorse any agency. We simply provide information as a public service.
